assumption_ledger.note: The current objective explicitly shifted from dashboard speed to safety and rollback confidence.
Visible evidence says schema drift tests already exist, but dependency-order fixtures are still missing.
The scenario does not specify the exact success criteria or duration required to declare the kill-switched dashboard canary proven.

dependency_notes.reason: The runbook should be updated only after ordering-regression coverage exists, otherwise the checklist documents an unverified safety path.
Fixture coverage reduces ambiguity around whether schema changes alter release ordering, making the schema audit decision evidence-backed.
Because rollback confidence is now the primary objective, operator guidance must be ready before exposing even a kill-switched dashboard canary.
The dashboard summary depends on a stable translator schema and an explicit decision about the legacy parser shim.
Enterprise change-window communication should cite proven canary behavior, not an unexercised rollout order.

ordered_steps.bounded_deliverable: Fixture-backed ordering coverage is added to the plan contract and the missing customer-facing safety gap is closed.
ordered_steps.summary: Land the dependency-graph fixtures that catch ordering regressions so the team has a concrete safety gate before any further rollout work.
ordered_steps.title: Backfill dependency-order fixtures and safety checks

ordered_steps.bounded_deliverable: Operators have an updated checklist and runbook that reflect the safe sequencing and rollback path for guarded change windows.
ordered_steps.summary: Refresh the operator-facing rollback and launch guidance immediately after fixture coverage lands, because the current objective now prioritizes safety and rollback confidence over dashboard speed.
ordered_steps.title: Update the operator runbook and launch checklist for rollback safety

ordered_steps.bounded_deliverable: A documented schema-drift decision is made with the shim retained or removed based on fixture-backed evidence rather than stale ordering assumptions.
ordered_steps.summary: Use the new fixture coverage and operator path to confirm the translator schema is stable before any legacy parser shim removal decision is made.
ordered_steps.title: Audit schema drift before touching the legacy parser shim

ordered_steps.bounded_deliverable: A kill-switched canary proves the summary path without exposing full rollout blast radius.
ordered_steps.summary: Enable the translated release-plan dashboard summary only after the fixture, operator, and schema guardrails are in place, and keep it behind a kill switch while validating user-visible ordering.
ordered_steps.title: Canary the translated dashboard summary behind a kill switch

ordered_steps.bounded_deliverable: Enterprise change-window communication goes out with a validated ordering story and rollback posture.
ordered_steps.summary: Communicate the corrected rollout order to enterprise customers only after the safe sequence is locked by fixtures, operator guidance, and canary evidence.
ordered_steps.title: Send the enterprise change-window update once the order is locked

mitigations: Make dependency-fixture backfill the first milestone so ordering regressions are caught before rollout work resumes.
Update the operator runbook before canary exposure so rollback and launch handling reflect the new safety-first objective.
Keep the dashboard summary behind a kill switch until fixture, schema, and operator prerequisites are complete.
primary_risk.statement: If dashboard or enterprise rollout steps happen before fixture-backed ordering checks and rollback guidance, customers can see the wrong release-plan order during guarded change windows and operators will not have a trusted recovery path.
